project_name: Popper - Neovim File Change Auto-Open Plugin
context: Neovim plugin in Lua using vim.loop (libuv bindings) for filesystem watching. Standard Neovim plugin layout: plugin/popper.lua (entry point), lua/popper/ (modules: init.lua with setup/start/stop, gitignore.lua with parse_gitignore/is_ignored, watcher.lua, tabs.lua). Testing via plenary.nvim test harness (busted-style spec files under spec/). Test runner command: nvim --headless -u spec/minimal_init.lua -c "PlenaryBustedDirectory spec/ {minimal_init = 'spec/minimal_init.lua'}" +quit. The -u flag is required to load plenary before invoking PlenaryBustedDirectory. Setup function accepts config with watch_dir (default vim.loop.cwd()) and poll_interval_ms (default 1000).
test_spec: 1. Given a Neovim session with popper started in a project directory, when a new file is created in a subdirectory (not gitignored), then that file opens in a new tab. 2. Given a file is already open in an existing tab, when that file is modified on disk, then the session switches to that existing tab instead of creating a duplicate. 3. Given a .gitignore file exists with patterns like 'node_modules/' and '*.log', when files matching those patterns are created or modified, then they are not opened by popper. 4. Given popper is running, when the user runs :PopperStop, then subsequent file changes do not trigger any tab openings. 5. Given popper is started with a custom config specifying a watch directory, when files change in that directory, then they are opened; files outside that directory are ignored.

stories.title: Plugin scaffolding and test harness
stories.description: Create the standard Neovim plugin directory structure: plugin/popper.lua (entry point that sources lua/popper/init.lua), lua/popper/init.lua (main module with setup function accepting a config table), and spec/minimal_init.lua (minimal init file for plenary.nvim test harness). The setup function in init.lua accepts a config table with optional keys: watch_dir (defaults to vim.loop.cwd()), poll_interval_ms (defaults to 1000). Create a basic spec/popper_spec.lua that verifies the setup function returns a table with start() and stop() methods.
acceptance_criteria: plugin/popper.lua exists and calls require('popper').setup() when loaded
lua/popper/init.lua exports a setup() function that returns a table with start() and stop() stub functions
spec/minimal_init.lua configures plenary.nvim test harness
Running the test runner command executes spec/popper_spec.lua with at least one passing test that verifies setup() returns start and stop functions

stories.title: Gitignore parser module
stories.description: Create lua/popper/gitignore.lua with a function parse_gitignore(path) that reads a .gitignore file and returns a list of Lua patterns. Create a function is_ignored(file_path, patterns, base_dir) that returns true if file_path matches any of the gitignore patterns relative to base_dir. Support basic gitignore syntax: literal directory names (dir/), glob patterns (*.log), negation patterns (!important.log), and recursive matching (**/vendor). Write spec/gitignore_spec.lua with tests covering: ignoring a directory pattern, ignoring a glob pattern, negation patterns, and paths that should not be ignored.
acceptance_criteria: lua/popper/gitignore.lua exports parse_gitignore() and is_ignored() functions
parse_gitignore() correctly converts 'node_modules/' to a pattern that matches paths containing node_modules
parse_gitignore() correctly converts '*.log' to a pattern matching files ending in .log
is_ignored('src/node_modules/foo.js', patterns, '/project') returns true when 'node_modules/' is in patterns
is_ignored('src/main.lua', patterns, '/project') returns false for patterns that only ignore node_modules and *.log
spec/gitignore_spec.lua contains at least 5 passing tests covering directory, glob, negation, and non-match cases

stories.title: Recursive file watcher with gitignore filtering
stories.description: Create lua/popper/watcher.lua with a module that uses vim.loop to recursively watch a directory tree. Implement start_watch(dir, gitignore_patterns, on_change_callback) which: (1) walks the directory tree using vim.loop.fs_scandir, (2) sets up vim.loop.fs_event watchers on each directory, (3) filters out gitignored paths using the gitignore module, (4) calls on_change_callback(absolute_path) when a file is created or modified. Implement stop_watch() which closes all fs_event handles. Write spec/watcher_spec.lua with tests that verify: callback is called when a watched file is modified, callback is not called for gitignored files, and stop_watch prevents further callbacks.
acceptance_criteria: lua/popper/watcher.lua exports start_watch() and stop_watch() functions
start_watch() uses vim.loop.fs_scandir to enumerate directories recursively
start_watch() uses vim.loop.fs_event to detect file changes
start_watch() filters paths through is_ignored() from gitignore.lua before invoking callback
stop_watch() closes all active fs_event handles and prevents further callbacks
spec/watcher_spec.lua contains at least 3 passing tests: callback on change, no callback for gitignored, stop prevents callbacks

stories.title: Tab management - open or switch to file
stories.description: Create lua/popper/tabs.lua with a function open_or_switch(file_path) that: (1) iterates all tabs using vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages(), (2) for each tab, checks its windows and buffers using vim.api.nvim_tabpage_list_wins() and vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(), (3) compares buffer names using vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name() against file_path, (4) if found, switches to that tab using vim.api.nvim_set_current_tabpage(), (5) if not found, opens the file in a new tab using vim.cmd('tabedit ' .. file_path). Write spec/tabs_spec.lua with tests verifying: switching to existing tab when file is already open, creating new tab when file is not open, and handling non-existent file paths gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: lua/popper/tabs.lua exports open_or_switch(file_path) function
When file_path matches a buffer name in an existing tab, vim.api.nvim_set_current_tabpage() is called with that tab
When file_path does not match any existing buffer, vim.cmd('tabedit ...') is called to open in a new tab
When file_path does not exist on disk, the function returns without error and does not call tabedit
spec/tabs_spec.lua contains at least 3 passing tests: switch to existing tab, open new tab, handle non-existent file

stories.title: Integration: wire watcher to tab opener with user commands
stories.description: Update lua/popper/init.lua to wire the watcher and tabs modules together. The setup() function stores config, and start() calls watcher.start_watch() with the config.watch_dir, parsed gitignore patterns from .gitignore in watch_dir, and a callback that calls tabs.open_or_switch(). stop() calls watcher.stop_watch(). Add Vim commands in plugin/popper.lua: :PopperStart calls require('popper').start(), :PopperStop calls require('popper').stop(). Add an autocmd on VimEnter to optionally auto-start if config.auto_start is true. Write spec/integration_spec.lua that mocks the watcher and tabs modules to verify: start() passes correct arguments to watcher, change callback invokes open_or_switch, and stop() calls watcher.stop_watch().
acceptance_criteria: lua/popper/init.lua start() calls watcher.start_watch() with watch_dir, gitignore patterns, and a callback that invokes tabs.open_or_switch()
lua/popper/init.lua stop() calls watcher.stop_watch()
plugin/popper.lua defines :PopperStart and :PopperStop user commands
:PopperStart calls require('popper').start() without error
:PopperStop calls require('popper').stop() without error
spec/integration_spec.lua contains at least 3 passing tests verifying the wiring between modules
Running the full test suite (all spec files) passes with zero failures
